title: Decision on how the European Commission handled a request for public access to documents exchanged by the Commission’s Task Force for Migration Management
text: Dear Mr X, You submitted a complaint to the European Ombudsman, on behalf of Y, about how the Commission dealt with a request for public access to documents exchanged by the Commission’s Task Force for Migration Management, registered under the reference EASE 2024/5047. The Ombudsman opened an inquiry into the Commission’s implicit refusal to give public access under Regulation 1049/2001. As a first inquiry step, the Ombudsman asked the Commission to reply to your confirmatory application and, in the absence of a reply, she asked to inspect the documents at issue. The Commission adopted its confirmatory decision on 1 December 2025. The Ombudsman notes that the Commission granted wider public access to three documents and confirmed that it does not hold further documents falling within the scope of the request. In your comments to the Ombudsman following the adoption of the Commission’s confirmatory decision, you did not take issue with the extent of public access now granted. Given that this inquiry concerned the Commission’s implicit refusal to give public access, the case is therefore closed with the conclusion that it has been settled . That said, in your comments to the Ombudsman, you reiterated your concern that the Commission fails to respect the applicable time limits to deal with periodic requests for public access to documents relating to the activities of the Task Force Migration Management. You also referred to the Ombudsman’s suggestion in case OI/3/2022/MHZ that the Commission should increase the overall transparency of the work of this task force. As mentioned when opening the inquiry into your complaint, the Ombudsman did not open an inquiry into the time taken by the Commission to adopt a confirmatory decision in this case, considering that she has already found the significant delays in processing public access requests to amount to maladministration. [1] However, the Ombudsman continues to monitor the matter on the basis of complaints submitted to her. She also continues to monitor the overall transparency of the Commission’s cooperation with national authorities in migration management, again on the basis of complaints [2] , and brought your concerns in this regard to the Commission’s attention. Yours sincerely, Rosita Hickey Director of Inquiries Strasbourg, 04/02/2026 [1] See decision of the Ombudsman in OI/2/2022/OAM available at: https://www.ombudsman.europa.eu/en/decision/en/175321 . [2] See, for instance, case 789/2024/PVV on the European Commission's refusal to give public access to documents concerning support for migration management in Cyprus: https://www.ombudsman.europa.eu/en/case/en/66312 ; case 2237/2024/AML on the European Commission's refusal to give public access to the minutes of meetings of the EU Regional Task Force Greece: https://www.ombudsman.europa.eu/en/case/en/67755 .
